content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: TSA/Security | San Jos\xE9 Mineta\
  \ International Airport\n- url: https://www.flysanjose.com/node/10161\n- source:\
  \ San Jos\xE9 Mineta International Airport\n- domain: flysanjose.com\n\n## Content\n\
  \n# TSA and Security Identification\n\n## Identification for domestic flights\n\n\
  Passengers age 18 and older must present either a REAL ID-compliant state driver\u2019\
  s license or identification card, a state-issued enhanced driver\u2019s license,\
  \ or another form of identification accepted by the Transportation Security Administration\
  \ at the airport security checkpoint.\n\nExamples of accepted alternatives include\
  \ a valid U.S. passport or passport card, a U.S. Department of Defense identification\
  \ card, a DHS Trusted Traveler card, certain Tribal identification documents, and\
  \ other credentials included on TSA\u2019s accepted-identification list. A traveler\
  \ does not need both a REAL ID-compliant state credential and an accepted alternative\
  \ document.\n\nSJC directs travelers to TSA for the complete list of accepted identification\
  \ because TSA controls the checkpoint identity requirements.\n\n## Travelers without\
  \ acceptable identification\n\nBeginning February 1, 2026, a traveler who has lost\
  \ an identification document or arrives without a REAL ID or another accepted form\
  \ of identification may use TSA ConfirmID. ConfirmID is a fee-based identity-verification\
  \ option. The fee is $45 and covers a period of up to 10 days from the travel date\
  \ shown on the receipt. Paying the fee does not guarantee that TSA will be able\
  \ to verify the traveler\u2019s identity.\n\nUsing TSA ConfirmID is optional. It\
  \ is intended for travelers who do not have an acceptable identification document.\
  \ A traveler who chooses not to use ConfirmID and cannot present an acceptable document\
  \ may be denied access to the security checkpoint and may miss the flight.\n\nTravelers\
  \ who need ConfirmID should allow additional time because the process may add approximately\
  \ 10 to 30 minutes. SJC notes that TSA recommends completing the payment process\
  \ in advance to reduce delays.\n\n## Passport and digital identification\n\nA U.S.\
  \ passport or passport card is listed as an acceptable alternative to a REAL ID-compliant\
  \ state credential for domestic checkpoint identification. SJC also describes an\
  \ Apple Digital ID option for eligible passengers who store U.S. passport information\
  \ in Apple Wallet. The digital option is a convenience feature; a physical passport\
  \ is still required for international travel.\n\n## TSA PreCheck and CLEAR\n\nTSA\
  \ PreCheck and CLEAR are separate programs that may provide a faster or more convenient\
  \ identity and screening experience for eligible or enrolled travelers. SJC provides\
  \ information about enrollment and operating locations, but these programs do not\
  \ replace the basic requirement to present an accepted form of identification unless\
  \ the traveler is using an eligible TSA-approved digital or touchless process.\n\
  \nCLEAR is available at the Terminal A and Terminal B checkpoints. Travelers may\
  \ enroll through CLEAR\u2019s own process. SJC also notes that CLEAR offers TSA\
  \ PreCheck enrollment and renewal services at the Terminal B checkpoint during posted\
  \ hours.\n\n## Check before traveling\n\nTravelers should confirm that the identification\
  \ they plan to carry is current and appears on TSA\u2019s accepted-identification\
  \ list. Someone who already has a valid accepted document, such as a valid U.S.\
  \ passport, does not need TSA ConfirmID merely because their state driver\u2019\
  s license is not REAL ID-compliant.\n"

title: "TSA/Security | San Jos\xE9 Mineta International Airport"
url: https://www.flysanjose.com/node/10161

You **do not need to complete the extra identity-verification service** before your flight.

Your trip is domestic from SJC, and you have a valid **U.S. passport** expiring September 14, 2031. A passport is an accepted alternative to a REAL ID-compliant state ID, so you can use it at the TSA security checkpoint even though you do not have a REAL ID state license.

Before leaving:

- Bring the **physical U.S. passport** with you.
- Use it at TSA instead of your state ID.
- Do **not** pay for or enroll in TSA ConfirmID; it is intended for travelers who lack an accepted identity document.
- Allow your normal recommended airport time.

SJC’s guidance confirms that travelers with a valid accepted document, such as a U.S. passport, do not need TSA ConfirmID merely because their state license is not REAL ID-compliant: [SJC TSA and Security Identification](https://www.flysanjose.com/node/10161).

final_answer:
You **do not need to complete the extra identity-verification service** before your flight.

Your trip is domestic from SJC, and you have a valid **U.S. passport** expiring September 14, 2031. A passport is an accepted alternative to a REAL ID-compliant state ID, so you can use it at the TSA security checkpoint even though you do not have a REAL ID state license.

Before leaving:

- Bring the **physical U.S. passport** with you.
- Use it at TSA instead of your state ID.
- Do **not** pay for or enroll in TSA ConfirmID; it is intended for travelers who lack an accepted identity document.
- Allow your normal recommended airport time.

SJC’s guidance confirms that travelers with a valid accepted document, such as a U.S. passport, do not need TSA ConfirmID merely because their state license is not REAL ID-compliant: [SJC TSA and Security Identification](https://www.flysanjose.com/node/10161).
